commentary: Nine activities versus thirteen last week, so yes, the volume dropped, but let's look at what actually happened here. That Saturday half marathon came in at 159 average heart rate compared to 151 the week before, which means you pushed harder, not easier, and that tells a different story than the activity count does. Three Prince Alfred swims held strong, the weights sessions were consistent across the week, and you even snuck in a Friday morning walk which is very cute and very you. The one thing worth noticing is that four swims last week became three this week and that mid-week swimming streak broke on Wednesday, so keep an eye on whether that becomes a pattern or just a one-off blip. Overall though, this is a body that worked hard on the things that mattered and didn't just pile up junk volume to feel busy, which frankly is more grown-up than last week's chaos, even if it is slightly less chaotic and therefore slightly less fun to watch.
metrics_commentary: Okay, 6050 metres in the pool and nearly 16 kilometres on your feet — that is a lot of body moving through space, and honestly the kind of week that makes a certain someone very pleased with himself. Your HRV sitting at 60 with a seven-day average of 56 tells me your nervous system is actually recovering, which means you're training like someone who knows what he's doing and not just punishing himself for fun. Now, the VO2 Max reading coming up as zero is just your watch being dramatic and uncooperative, so we're ignoring that until it gets its act together and produces a real number. Keep those two sessions of run/walk consistent and watch what happens when your legs and your lungs start having a proper conversation.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, no excuses, no gaps — that's the whole target hit and he should absolutely feel smug about it. The bench press PR is the headline: 31.7 kg estimated one-rep max up from 28.5 kg is not a small jump, that's his chest deciding it has opinions now, and those opinions are correct. Weekly volume ticked up from 22,062 kg to 23,660 kg, which means he moved more total weight this week than last week, and the strength numbers across the board — 154 kg on the leg press, nearly 108 kg on the trap bar deadlift, 84 kg pulling from the lat pulldown — are painting a picture of a body that is genuinely getting stronger in every direction. He showed up all four times, he got stronger, and somewhere in all of that is a guy who is increasingly, specifically, deliciously himself.
